prompt: Build a ChargeOps Pricing & Warehouse Administration API that runs fully offline to manage EV charging pricing, operational configuration, and master data for multi-organization operators. The service exposes Authentication and Identity APIs that support registration, login/logout, session refresh, and password recovery using locally generated recovery tokens (no email/SMS); passwords must be at least 12 characters, include 3 of 4 character classes, and are stored as salted hashes. Authorization is enforced through User/Permission APIs with role-based access control for Guest, User, Merchant, and Administrator; merchants can manage only their own stations/devices and pricing templates, while administrators can manage all organizations, users, and global configurations. Billing & Pricing Strategy APIs support defining price templates per station or device with combined components (energy in kWh, duration in minutes, and fixed service fee), time-of-use windows (e.g., weekday 7:00 AM–10:00 AM peak), and sales tax flags; templates are versioned with effective start timestamps, activation, deactivation, and rollback, and orders must be recalculable against the price version active at the order’s start time. Operations Configuration and Notification Reach APIs allow configuring content modules (carousel slots, campaign placements, hot rankings) as structured records with priority, start/end time, and target audience role, plus an offline message center with subscription policies (quiet hours 9:00 PM–7:00 AM local time, max 2 messages/day per user per template, and per-template opt-out). Delivery effectiveness analytics must track generated, delivered-to-inbox, opened, and dismissed counts without relying on push services. Data is persisted in PostgreSQL with sqlx-managed transactions, and the Go Echo backend enforces request validation and permission checks consistently across all resource domains. Core data models include organizations (hierarchy via parent_id, unique org_code), warehouses with zones and bins (unique bin_code within a warehouse), item dictionary (standardized item_name, sku, category_id), units of measure with conversion factors (decimal(18,6), constrained to positive), and supplier/carrier masters with duplicate checks using normalized name + tax_id where present. Pricing models include station/device identifiers, template versions (monotonic version_number per template), time-of-use rules (non-overlapping windows per day type), and immutable order pricing snapshots referencing the applied version for audit. Security requirements include device login-state management with per-device session records, idle session timeout at 30 minutes and absolute expiration at 7 days, account lockout for 15 minutes after 10 failed attempts in 10 minutes, and field-level masking for tax_id and any stored address lines. Non-functional requirements target single-node Docker deployment, 99th-percentile API latency under 300 ms for typical queries at 50 RPS on commodity hardware, full audit trails for admin/merchant configuration changes, and offline observability via structured logs and local metrics tables exportable as files without external collectors.
